prototype_structure_type: High-entropy monoclinic C2/m (ccp Cl, stacking faults)
rationale: High configurational entropy (5 M-cation species) promotes stacking faults and 3D diffusion vs. 2D in Li3InCl6. Ionic potential descriptor guides phase stability. 2.13 mS/cm > 1.27 mS/cm for Li3InCl6. Multiple additional HE compositions unexplored.

rationale: Low Hf content (x=0.3) enhances conductivity 28% over Li3InCl6 without sacrificing stability window (2.68-4.22 V). Aliovalent substitution creates extra Li vacancies. ccp Cl sublattice with monoclinic C2/m. Excellent candidate for further optimization x=0.2-0.5.

rationale: Systematic ML-MD and experiments show maximum conductivity in Li3YCl6-xBrx at x≈4.5. Cl/Br site disorder significantly expands diffusion pathways versus pure endpoints. Band gap ~5.3 eV exceeds threshold. Composition is underexplored beyond endpoints.

activation_energy_eV: <0.30 likely; not exact in extracted table

rationale: Si4+ introduces Li+ excess (charge balance) boosting carrier density. 1.7 mS/cm confirmed experimentally. I- anion keeps bottleneck wide. Lower cost than Ge variant. Underexplored in terms of optimal Si content beyond x=0.25.

rationale: LiMXCl4 family exhibits soft-cradle effect: MX2Cl4 octahedral chains tilt in concert with Li hops. LiHfFCl4 Li-stuffed variant predicted 137.5 mS/cm—highest in family. F-for-O substitution widens electrochemical window. E_hull 12-32 meV/atom depending on competing phases. Synthesis feasibility not yet proven experimentally.

The Gd-I6 analogue gives 0.59 mS/cm; Br3I3 mix gives 13x improvement via disorder and bottleneck tuning. Na halide electrolytes remain far less explored than Li analogues.

Mixing In+Er retunes structure toward ccp. Analogous strategy proved successful for Y1-xInxCl6 reaching ~1 mS/cm at x≥0.5.

prototype_structure_type: Cubic I-43m (BCC-like; As at P site)

prototype_structure_type: Trigonal (P-3m1, hcp Cl; Hf4+ analogue of Zr)
stability_window_V: 0.6-4.3 (est. from Li2ZrCl6 analogue; Hf4+ d0 wider gap)
rationale: Hf4+ has essentially same ionic radius and charge as Zr4+ but heavier mass and potentially wider band gap. Li2HfCl6 expected similar hcp structure to Li2ZrCl6 but with wider electrochemical window. No conductivity study found in literature—gap to fill.

graphite_compatibility: Moderate - graphite with interlayer; reductive limit ~0.6 V near graphite
lini_o2_compatibility: Moderate - LiNiO2 borderline at 4.3 V
lico_o2_compatibility: Good - LiCoO2 within estimated window
